# Shkiah

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Sea Level Sunset

Sunset calculated as if the location were at sea level, without any elevation adjustment.

This is the sea-level sunset time. It is used as the base for dusk calculations that are measured as the sun’s dip below the horizon after sunset.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `AstronomicalCalendar.getSeaLevelSunset`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.AstronomicalCalendar.getSeaLevelSunset`

## Sunset

Sunset adjusted for the location’s elevation.

The calculation uses a geometric zenith of 90° plus the elevation adjustment. It also accounts for about 34 arcminutes of refraction and 16 arcminutes for the sun’s radius, for a total of 90.83333°.

In certain cases, sunset can be calculated before sunrise, typically when a time zone other than the local time zone is used. In that case, the sunset date is moved to the following date.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `AstronomicalCalendar.getSunset`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.AstronomicalCalendar.getSunset`

## Sunset Or Westernmost Solar Azimuth

Sunset when it occurs, or otherwise the time when the sun reaches its westernmost position, at azimuth 270°.

In polar regions, there are days with no sunrise or sunset. Some opinions use the sun’s westernmost position as the day-night boundary during these periods. This time is close to six hours after astronomical chatzos hayom, but depending on the time of year and location in the Arctic or Antarctic, it can be up to 46 minutes before or after that time.

The opinion described is attributed to [Rabbi Yehosef Schwarz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Schwarz_(geographer)) in [דברי יוסף – דרך מבוא השמש](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=31703&pgnum=134) and [דברי יוסף – תשובות, שאלה ח׳](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=159&pgnum=83), and is brought down lehalacha by the [Ben Ish Chai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yosef_Hayyim) in [רב פעלים – חלק ב׳, סוד ישרים ס׳ ד׳](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=1401&pgnum=461).

If there is no sunset that day and azimuth 270° also does not occur, this time may not be available.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSunsetOrWesternmostSolarAzimuth`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSunsetOrWesternmostSolarAzimuth`
